name: Guillena Water Route Trail
name_original: Embalse del Gergal — circular desde Guillena
description: This moderate 7.1-mile (11.5 km) jogging route around Embalse del Gergal offers scenic reservoir views and Mediterranean forest trails.
region_intro: Jogging around Guillena offers diverse natural landscapes at the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountains, just 22 kilometers from Seville. The region is characterized by Mediterranean forests, including holm oaks and cork oaks, and features several rivers like the Huelva and Cala, which feed into picturesque reservoirs. Joggers can explore well-maintained paths along riverbanks, through forests, and around the El Gergal Peri-urban Park, providing varied terrain for all fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Guillena The most popular jogging route is Embalse del Gergal — circular from Guillena, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Gergal Reservoir – Guillena Footbridge loop from Urbanización Entremontes, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This challenging route explores the wider area around the reservoir and river. Local runners also love the The Pines — Gergal Reservoir – Waterway Path Bridge loop from Guillena, a 14.1 miles (22.6 km) trail leading through pine forests and along the reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Jogging around Guillena is defined by its Sierra Morena foothills, tranquil river paths, and extensive Mediterranean forests surrounding reservoirs. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy family-friendly paths to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Guillena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Guillena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Guillena?
A: Guillena offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 60 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy paths to more challenging long-distance runs.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Guillena?
A: Yes, Guillena has several easy and beginner-friendly jogging paths. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012797263/embalse-del-gergal-circular-from-guillena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Gergal — circular from Guillena&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.1-mile (11.5 km) route that offers scenic views around the reservoir and is a popular choice. The Water Route also has a 5 km section considered very simple and family-friendly.

Q: What are some options for longer running trails in Guillena?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir – Guillena Footbridge loop from Urbanización Entremontes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.9-mile (38.5 km) path that explores the wider area around the reservoir and river. Another excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pines — Gergal Reservoir – Waterway Path Bridge loop from Guillena&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.1-mile (22.6 km) trail through pine forests.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Guillena?
A: Absolutely. Many of Guillena's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012797263/embalse-del-gergal-circular-from-guillena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Gergal — circular from Guillena&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop around the reservoir.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on Guillena's running trails?
A: Guillena's running trails boast diverse and beautiful scenery. You'll encounter Mediterranean forests dominated by holm oaks and gall oaks, picturesque riverbanks along the Huelva and Cala rivers, and stunning views around the El Gergal reservoir. The region's position at the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountains also provides varied terrain and scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any points of interest or attractions to see while jogging in Guillena?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of interesting landmarks. Along the Water Route, you might spot historical elements like old railway stations and villages. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are highlights themselves. For broader attractions, consider visiting the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2616147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Columbus Monument&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4081520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amphitheatre of Italica&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are Guillena's running routes suitable for families?
A: Many of Guillena's paths are suitable for families, especially those looking for a gentle jog or walk. The Water Route, for example, includes a 5 km section that is described as 'very simple' and family-friendly, offering a pleasant nature adventure through stunning landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Guillena?
A: The running routes in Guillena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the beautiful scenery around the reservoirs and rivers.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Guillena?
A: Guillena's climate makes it suitable for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. While summer can be hot, early mornings or late evenings are still viable. Winter running can be quite enjoyable with cooler, crisp air, especially along the river paths and through the Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there any specific natural features or wildlife I might encounter?
A: While running in Guillena, you'll be immersed in Mediterranean forests with holm oaks, gall oaks, pines, and cork oaks. The Huelva and Cala rivers, along with the reservoirs, create rich ecosystems. Keep an eye out for local birdlife and other small animals that inhabit these natural spaces, especially in areas like the El Gergal Peri-urban Park and La Zahurdilla Natural Park.

Q: How accessible are the running routes from Guillena town center?
A: Many routes are easily accessible from Guillena's town center. For instance, the El Gergal Peri-urban Park is just over a kilometer away, connected by an avenue that includes a promenade, making it convenient for joggers to reach the trails.

Q: Are there routes that offer a mix of forest and water views?
A: Yes, many routes in Guillena perfectly combine forest and water elements. The Water Route itself passes by the Huelva and Cala rivers, offering riverside paths and forest sections. Trails around the El Gergal reservoir, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pines — Gergal Reservoir – Waterway Path Bridge loop from Guillena&lt;/a&gt;, lead through pine forests and along the water's edge, providing a beautiful blend of landscapes.
tips: Small footbridge that crosses the Rivera de Huelva on its way through the town of Guillena. This is almost often full of water in a beautiful natural space!
Reservoir at the confluence of the Rivera de Huelva river with the Rivera de Cala river. Its exploitation began in 1979.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Be aware that after heavy rainfall, sections of the unpaved path can become wet or even waterlogged, so appropriate footwear is essential. The full circular route might also be impassable if the Gergal Reservoir is completely full.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse del Gergal — circular from Guillena trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts with about 2 kilometers of paved sidewalk and a bike lane as you leave Guillena. It then transitions to unpaved dirt tracks and paths that wind through a beautiful Mediterranean forest. While generally accessible, some sections can be uneven, and as mentioned, may get muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While rated as 'moderate' for jogging due to its length and some varied terrain, the trail is generally considered accessible. The initial paved section is very easy, and the overall elevation gain is relatively small. Families looking for a tranquil outdoor experience will find it suitable, though younger children might find the full 11.5 km challenging.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Embalse del Gergal — circular from Guillena trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other visitors, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main attraction is the stunning Gergal Reservoir, which serves as the centerpiece. You'll also cross the Guillena Footbridge and pass through the Parque Periurbano El Gergal, characterized by Mediterranean forest. On clear days, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Guadalquivir valley, Guillena, and even Seville in the distance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts directly from Guillena, making it easily accessible. You can typically find parking within the town of Guillena, especially near the initial promenade section, which integrates with the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Parque Periurbano El Gergal?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Parque Periurbano El Gergal or to jog this circular route. It's a publicly accessible natural area.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Ruta del Agua, Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, and Cordel de la Cruz de la Mujer, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Guillena, you'll find various cafes and restaurants in the town where you can refuel before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a typical Mediterranean forest ecosystem with holm oaks, cork oaks, pines, and poplars, alongside scrubland like palmitos and lavender. The Gergal Reservoir itself is a significant feature, and after heavy rains, the dam can create a dramatic waterfall.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this route typically takes around 1 hour and 17 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.
